title: Endressia pyrenaica
taxonomy: Endressia pyrenaica is classified within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It belongs to the genus Endressia, which is a small group of flowering plants. The species is native to the Pyrenees mountain range, giving it its specific name. Endressia pyrenaica is further classified as a perennial herb, with a specific growth habit and leaf structure. Its classification is part of the larger botanical nomenclature system, which helps scientists and botanists identify and study plant species.

intro: Endressia pyrenaica is a flowering plant species in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the Pyrenean angelica or Pyrenean angelica. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Endressia pyrenaica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Endressia pyrenaica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support respiratory health and aid in the management of various inflammatory conditions.
